ogTitle: Gnowellen vs Goode Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gnowellen and Goode Beach.
heading: Gnowellen vs Goode Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gnowellen, Western Australia and Goode Beach, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Goode Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Gnowellen (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gnowellen or Goode Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gnowellen has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Goode Beach scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gnowellen scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Goode Beach scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gnowellen or Goode Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Goode Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
